name: Teriyaki Meatballs
recipeIngredient: 1 tablespoon finely chopped crystallized or fresh ginger
2 tablespoons finely chopped crystallized or fresh ginger
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine pork, water chestnuts, green onions, 1 tablespoon ginger, salt, 3 tablespoons soy sauce, and eggs in a large bowl. Mix well with hands.
2. Add bread crumbs and mix until just combined. Chill the mixture.
3. Shape into 3/4 to 1 inch balls and roll them in cornstarch.
4. Brown meat on all sides in hot oil. Remove the balls and put them on a roasting pan.
5. To freeze, place the meatballs in a single layer on a baking sheet and then transfer to freezer bags when frozen. Use as needed.
6. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and bake meatballs for 15 to 20 minutes until cooked through.
7. To make sauce, combine pineapple with juice, vinegar, 1/4 cup soy sauce, 2/3 cup sugar, beef broth, and ginger in a large saucepan. Bring to boiling.
8. Mix cornstarch with cold water, then add it to the boiling mixture while stirring constantly.
9. Continue cooking until the sauce is thick and clear. The sauce will keep in the refrigerator for up to one week.
10. To serve, place meatballs in a chafing dish with enough sauce to coat them. Serve with toothpicks.
